mechanism_narrative: Rising short-term interest rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate increases with p-value < 0.05, or rolling Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 65% of triggered months, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a logistic regression controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and construction activity, which reduces corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline MoM
failure_condition: Joint declines in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict negative 60-day returns (logistic coefficient > 0) or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index reading above its 12-month moving average confirms robust manufacturing output that supports near-term corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 45% of instances where industrial production exceeds its trend, or the signal reverses direction during high volatility regimes (VIX > 25).

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS declines faster than CPIAUCSL inflation, real borrowing costs fall, which expands equity valuation multiples and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The real rate spread fails to predict positive returns when inflation expectations exceed 3%, or the interaction term loses significance (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in total nonfarm payrolls directly reduce aggregate income and consumer purchasing power, which systematically lowers corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll declines are followed by positive 60-day returns in > 60% of observations, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested sub-periods.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate exceeding 2.5% signals strong economic momentum that boosts investor confidence and corporate profit forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in > 55% of high-growth quarters, or the signal shows no predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) after controlling for prior month market returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production during a liquidity crunch signals severe credit constraints on manufacturers, which forces earnings downgrades and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during stress periods are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the predictive coefficient flips sign when TED spread exceeds 1.5%.
